title: Set up Nginx as a reverse proxy for a Node.js / Next.js app
summary: Complete Nginx setup — install, server blocks, reverse proxy to Node.js, SSL via Certbot, WebSocket support, and gzip/caching headers.
declaration: I will configure Nginx as a reverse proxy in front of your Node.js or Next.js app. This includes HTTPS via Let's Encrypt/Certbot, WebSocket upgrade headers, and production-ready gzip settings. Tell me your domain name and the port your app listens on.
instructions:
## Set up Nginx as a reverse proxy for a Node.js / Next.js app

### 1. Install Nginx
- **Ubuntu / Debian**:
```bash
sudo apt update
sudo apt install nginx -y
sudo systemctl enable nginx
sudo systemctl start nginx
```
- **CentOS / RHEL / Amazon Linux 2**:
```bash
sudo yum install nginx -y   # or dnf on AL2023
sudo systemctl enable nginx
sudo systemctl start nginx
```
- Verify: `curl http://localhost` — should return the default Nginx welcome page.

### 2. Understand the config layout
- Main config: `/etc/nginx/nginx.conf`
- Site configs: `/etc/nginx/sites-available/` (Ubuntu) or `/etc/nginx/conf.d/` (RHEL)
- Enabled sites: `/etc/nginx/sites-enabled/` (symlinks on Ubuntu)
- IMPORTANT: On Ubuntu, do NOT edit `nginx.conf` directly — create per-site files in `sites-available/`.

### 3. Create a server block (HTTP first)
- Create `/etc/nginx/sites-available/myapp`:
```nginx
upstream app {
    server 127.0.0.1:3000;  # your Node.js / Next.js port
    keepalive 64;
}

server {
    listen 80;
    server_name example.com www.example.com;

    # Pass all requests to the Node.js app
    location / {
        proxy_pass         http://app;
        proxy_http_version 1.1;

        # Required headers
        proxy_set_header   Host              $host;
        proxy_set_header   X-Real-IP         $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header   X-Forwarded-For   $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header   X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;

        # WebSocket support
        proxy_set_header   Upgrade    $http_upgrade;
        proxy_set_header   Connection "upgrade";

        # Timeouts
        proxy_read_timeout 60s;
        proxy_connect_timeout 10s;
    }
}
```
- Enable the site:
```bash
sudo ln -s /etc/nginx/sites-available/myapp /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/
sudo nginx -t          # test config — must say "syntax is ok"
sudo systemctl reload nginx
```

### 4. Obtain SSL certificate with Certbot
- Install Certbot:
```bash
sudo apt install certbot python3-certbot-nginx -y   # Ubuntu
# or: sudo dnf install certbot python3-certbot-nginx -y  # RHEL
```
- Obtain and install certificate (Certbot automatically edits your server block):
```bash
sudo certbot --nginx -d example.com -d www.example.com
```
- Follow prompts — select option to redirect HTTP to HTTPS when asked.
- Certbot adds HTTPS server block and sets up an auto-renewal cron/systemd timer automatically.
- Verify renewal: `sudo certbot renew --dry-run`

### 5. Hardened HTTPS config (after Certbot)
- Certbot rewrites your file. Review and tune the SSL section:
```nginx
# Add inside the HTTPS server block Certbot creates:
ssl_protocols       TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3;
ssl_ciphers         HIGH:!aNULL:!MD5;
ssl_prefer_server_ciphers on;
ssl_session_cache   shared:SSL:10m;
ssl_session_timeout 10m;

# HSTS (only add after confirming HTTPS works end-to-end)
add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains" always;
add_header X-Content-Type-Options nosniff always;
add_header X-Frame-Options SAMEORIGIN always;
```

### 6. Enable gzip compression
- Add inside the `http {}` block in `/etc/nginx/nginx.conf` (or in your site config):
```nginx
gzip on;
gzip_vary on;
gzip_proxied any;
gzip_comp_level 6;
gzip_types text/plain text/css text/xml application/json application/javascript
           application/rss+xml application/atom+xml image/svg+xml;
```

### 7. Serve Next.js static assets directly (optional performance boost)
- Next.js outputs static files to `.next/static/`. Serving them directly via Nginx skips Node.js:
```nginx
# Inside your server block, before the location / block:
location /_next/static/ {
    alias /home/deploy/myapp/.next/static/;
    expires 1y;
    add_header Cache-Control "public, immutable";
    access_log off;
}

location /public/ {
    alias /home/deploy/myapp/public/;
    expires 7d;
    access_log off;
}
```
- IMPORTANT: The trailing slash on `alias` paths is required. `/home/deploy/myapp/.next/static/` not `/home/deploy/myapp/.next/static`.

### 8. Configure process manager (PM2 or systemd)
- Nginx only proxies — your Node.js app must stay running independently.
- **PM2** (recommended for Node.js):
```bash
npm install -g pm2
pm2 start npm --name myapp -- start
pm2 startup   # generates systemd unit to start PM2 on boot
pm2 save
```
- **systemd** alternative — create `/etc/systemd/system/myapp.service`:
```ini
[Unit]
Description=My Node.js App
After=network.target

[Service]
User=deploy
WorkingDirectory=/home/deploy/myapp
ExecStart=/usr/bin/node server.js
Restart=always
RestartSec=5
Environment=NODE_ENV=production
EnvironmentFile=/home/deploy/myapp/.env.production

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```
```bash
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable myapp
sudo systemctl start myapp
```

### 9. Open firewall ports
```bash
sudo ufw allow 80/tcp
sudo ufw allow 443/tcp
sudo ufw allow 22/tcp   # keep SSH open!
sudo ufw enable
```
- IMPORTANT: Allow SSH (port 22) BEFORE enabling ufw, or you will lock yourself out.

### 10. Verify end-to-end
- Check Nginx is running: `sudo systemctl status nginx`
- Check your app is running: `pm2 list` or `sudo systemctl status myapp`
- Test HTTP → HTTPS redirect: `curl -I http://example.com` — expect `301` to `https://`
- Test HTTPS: `curl -I https://example.com` — expect `200`
- Check SSL grade: https://www.ssllabs.com/ssltest/

### Common errors & fixes
- **`nginx: [emerg] bind() to 0.0.0.0:80 failed (98: Address already in use)`** — Another process (Apache, another Nginx instance) is on port 80. Run `sudo ss -tlnp | grep :80` to find it and stop it.
- **`502 Bad Gateway`** — Nginx is running but your Node.js app is not, or it crashed. Check `pm2 list` or `sudo systemctl status myapp` and review app logs.
- **`504 Gateway Timeout`** — The app is taking too long. Increase `proxy_read_timeout` in the Nginx config.
- **`curl: (60) SSL certificate problem`** — Certificate not yet issued or domain DNS hasn't propagated. Verify `dig example.com` points to your server's IP before running Certbot.
- **WebSocket connections dropping** — Missing `Upgrade` and `Connection` headers in the proxy config. Ensure both `proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade` and `proxy_set_header Connection "upgrade"` are present.
- **Static files return 403** — Wrong permissions on the `.next/static/` directory. Run `chmod -R 755 /home/deploy/myapp/.next/` and ensure the `nginx` user can read it.
- **`Too many redirects` in browser** — Your app is setting an HTTP redirect and Nginx is also redirecting. Ensure the app trusts `X-Forwarded-Proto` and does not issue its own HTTPS redirect when behind a proxy.
- **Certbot renewal fails** — Port 80 must be open to Let's Encrypt during renewal. Ensure `ufw` allows port 80 even if you redirect to HTTPS.

### What NOT to do
- Do NOT expose your Node.js port (3000) directly to the internet — only Nginx should be publicly reachable; bind Node.js to `127.0.0.1`.
- Do NOT skip `nginx -t` before reloading — a syntax error will take down all sites on the server.
- Do NOT disable HTTP (port 80) entirely — Certbot needs it for ACME challenge renewal.
- Do NOT add HSTS before confirming HTTPS works end-to-end — once browsers cache HSTS, HTTP becomes permanently inaccessible for your domain.
- Do NOT run your Node.js app as root — use a dedicated `deploy` user with minimal permissions.
- Do NOT store secrets in the Nginx config — use an `EnvironmentFile` in your systemd unit or pass them through PM2's ecosystem config.
- Do NOT use `proxy_pass http://localhost:3000` without an `upstream` block when you need `keepalive` — the `upstream` block is required for connection reuse.
